instructions: Remove the `# noqa: PLR0913` bypass from `_augment_recovery_message` in `src/jitsu/core/executor.py`. To resolve the underlying 'too many arguments' error natively, group the verification-related parameters (`summary`, `trimmed`, `failed_cmd`, `failing_file`) into a new Pydantic model or standard Dataclass named `VerificationFailureDetails` (or similar) within the appropriate models file. Update the method signature and all calling locations to pass this single object. Update tests accordingly.
anti_patterns: STRICTLY FORBIDDEN: Do not add any new `# noqa` or `# type: ignore` comments to bypass the refactor.
completion_criteria: The `# noqa: PLR0913` comment is completely removed.
The method signature has 5 or fewer arguments.
The test suite passes and coverage remains at 100%.

instructions: Perform a global sweep of the `src/jitsu/` directory and `tests/` directory. Find and remove EVERY SINGLE instance of `# noqa` or `# type: ignore`. You must fix the underlying code to naturally pass the Ruff and Pyright linters. If a line is too long, wrap it properly. If a type is ambiguous, use an explicit type cast or `assert`. Ensure the codebase relies on zero linting bypasses.
anti_patterns: STRICTLY FORBIDDEN: Do not change the underlying logic or behavior of the application; only change the structural syntax to satisfy the linters.
completion_criteria: Zero `# noqa` or `# type: ignore` strings exist anywhere in the repository.
